What an absolute failure of a game. Ever since I heard about this game during the early development, I was so excited. I played the beta and it was a lot of fun. I could see the potential for this game. Then they launched the game and it was a disaster. The beta was more playable then the release of the game. The biggest downfall of this game was that they released it way too early. So many prevalent ground breaking bugs, not adding cross play for months as it was advertised as a cross play game, people finding "mechanics" but in reality it was abusing a bunch of glitches, and so much more. Keep releasing your 200th collab for the in game purchases. If they had just delayed the game and fixed their game, they would actually have a community of people playing. Good Job Sloclap. You had an amazing thing on your hands and you threw it away because of greed.

if they stick with it this could be a great game. I'm having/had a lot of fun with it. However I def can't recommend it in the state its in right now the netcode is absolutely atrocious. This combined with their priority system often leaves you feeling like you've been robbed. This also allows for situations where people can just lob the ball into the box from the other side of the field and the opposing keeper basically just has to sit there and pray the server is on their side and they can knock it away since 9.5/10 times the volley will pass right through their dive. I probably have at least 15 clips of the ball just going right through me into the net as keeper and at least half of those will show the ball actually in my hands for a second complete with the sound of a save being made
Some other minor nitpicks: 4 total comm options which don't at all let me communicate with my team in the way id actually like to without voice, lack of other ranked options beside 5v5.
I hope to be able to return in a few months and rewrite this because beneath all the garbage netcode is a very fun game
ГОООООООООЛ

[h1]Идея на 5 — реализация на 3. Жёлтую карточку вам, разработчики[/h1]

Насколько я не люблю футбол, настолько эта игра мне понравилась. Я не знаком с прошлыми закрытыми тестами, и судя по отзывам люди разочаровались — многие проблемы и ошибки не были учтены и не исправлены к этому [i]"релизу"[/i]. И я как абсолютный новичок и радовался, и возмущался играя в данный продукт.

Я могу дать скидку на то, что это новый продукт и многое из желаемого разработчиками будет реализовано позднее. Но как же я устал жить в мире, где статус [b]релизная продукция[/b] уже ничего не значит в настоящее время. Кто в наши дни ожидает полностью готовый и рабочий продукт со всем контентом? И также можно рассказать про REMATCH, к сожалению.

[hr][/hr]

[h2]Сюжет, графика и звуки[/h2]

Как в любой типичной футбольной соревновательной аркаде (такой вот сюр) — сюжет здесь отсутствует. Вы, конечно, можете посмотреть пролог и заодно получить азы геймплея, но в целом ничего сверхъестественного. Вы обычный молодой пацан, которые соло пинает мяч о стенку. Играете местные мелкие турики и вас замечает тренер. Вас наняли в крупный ФК и вы как ЧСВ дальше играете соло. Но вас быстро сажают к запасным, а после усердных тренировок вы все таки забили этот несчастный гол. Тренер лыбится, мы смотрим на него как котенок на маму-кошку — конец.
На этом сюжет кончается.

[h3]Графика[/h3] здесь приемлемая. Не отличная, не плохая, она просто есть и её достаточно для такой игры. Конечно, это не последние FIFA или PES, у которых проработка может больше похвастаться, но всё же приятная глазу и мне её хватает. Конечно есть куда стремиться. Я не понимаю для чего мне кастомизация лица, если во время матча моё лицо такое же мыльное, как если бы я ничего не менял. В целом всё ок: протезы можно поставить, всякие болезни кожи — без проблем. Хочешь играть за очень и очень афроамериканца, пожалуйста. А хочешь за альбиноса — без проблем бро. Это и хорошо, и плохо.
Игра позиционирует себя больше к рейтинговой составляющей, чем к аркадной. Но на поле играют лысые, белые как айсберг деды, или полу-роботы, или паралимпийцы. Какой-то футбольный дух теряется, честно-говоря.

[h3]Звуки[/h3] мне понравились. В целом окружающая обстановка живая. Мячик слышно, трибуны кричат, саундтрек подстраивается. До FIFA еще конечно далеко, но в целом всё ОК.

[hr][/hr]

[h2]Геймплей[/h2]
Я знаю про футбол только базу, всякие мелкие правила мимо. Но даже меня, не любителя футбола — эта игра очень сильно затянула. У неё есть своеобразная фишка и мне она очень нравится. Но если вы не знаете правила футбола, они вам не понадобятся, потому что их здесь нет.

Пинаете мяч, пинаете мяч союзникам, пинаете мяч в ворота, отбираете мяч у соперников. Вот и вся база. Мяч за границу поля не улетит, здесь есть полу видимые стенки, от которых мяч может оттолкнуться. Это нечто новое и такое можно применять в тактическом плане. В игре отсутствуют понятия желтые/красные карточки, оффсайд, штрафной, угловой или пенальти. А жаль, это привнесло бы больше разнообразия в геймплей. Я бы с удовольствием провёл бы серию пенальти, или ударил бы через стенку со штрафного удара. К сожалению, но игра ощущается пустой. Тут есть финты, дриблинг и возможность подката. Это всё. Бежим, наводим курсор мыши в место, куда хотим отправить мяч — зажимаем ЛКМ и производим удар. Это вы будете делать 20% геймплея. Еще 50% вы будете бегать туда-сюда просто так. Еще 20% вы будете стоять у ворот и принимать мяч удерживая Ctrl, оставшиеся 10% исполнять суету, обводить и обманывать соперников, а также летать по полю по время подката. На этом весь геймплей. Пустовато, хотя казалось мне, что на релизе будет много всего. Разумеется не всё так просто, как я описал. Вам нужно включать голову, чтобы начать играть как минимум СРЕДНЕ. Нельзя просто бежать и забивать голы. Мячи у вас будут отнимать, вратарь хорошо ловить, а союзники недовольствоваться соло игрой. Игра скиллозависимая, поэтому, учитесь.
Я действительно ожидал чуть большего в корне геймплея, но кто я такой, чтобы чего-то ожидать от релиза игры в 2025...

[hr][/hr]

[h2]Баги, лаги и заключение[/h2]

Куда же без них. Багов, кстати, каких-то серьезных я не заметил. А вот лагов... Я не знаю на каких серверах находится игра (видимо на картошке GLaDOS), но на релизе меня отключало от серверов каждые 20 секунд. Я даже персонажа не мог настроить в меню, и у меня ушло 35 минут (!) на его кастомизацию. Но после установки GearUpBooster, оказывается, все проблемы решаются. Уж не знаю в чем проблема, разработчики недолюбливают Россию, или наши органы что-то где-то заблокировали, из-за чего я не мог стабильно держать соединение без всяких VеликихPерсональныхNастроек. Тем не менее, в самой игре пинг действительно решает. Мои 60 пинга ничто, против тех, кто сидит попой на серваке. Мяч телепортируется; я могу отобрать мяч и отбежать от соперника уже без него; будучи вратарем ловить мячи у меня на экране, но через секунду видеть 0-1 в счёт соперника, а на повторе наблюдать, что ты прыгнул и мяч пролетел сквозь мои руки. В этом плане эта игра очень сильно бесит. Я не могу нормально играть и четко координировать свои действия, просто потому что фризы, телепорты и вылеты — это база релизных игр 2025 года.

[h3]Заключение[/h3]
Карандашом я поставлю этой игре 7/10, просто потому, что эта игра привносит что-то новенькое в футбольную составляющую соревновательных игр. Она очень затягивает, что аж глаза болят. Но я надеюсь, что игра будет геометрически развиваться ЗА ТАКУЮ-ТО СТОИМОСТЬ. Я переплатил за про-издание, чтобы за 3 дня до релиза, все эти 3 дня ждать патчей. Если прогресса не будет — жаль, это станет ещё одним многообещающим проектом без должного развития.

[b]REMATCH — сырой продукт с великим задатком[/b]
Ufak tefek bugları var ama parasının hakkını sonuna kadar veriyor. Arkadaşlarınız yoksa çok bulaşmayın derim. Kendini ronaldinho sananlar geliyor ve oyun keyfinizin içine ediyor..

Rocket League without the cars. The only thing standing in Rematches way of being as popular as the former is the net code. After full release, the current build of the game is greatly improved, but there are still stutters here and there. If you were ever into the "street" series of sports games coming up, Rematch is worth checking out. It's a fun, competitive pvp soccer game that doesn't take itself too seriously. I do wish there was voice chat between teams post scoring or after the game to spice it up a bit, but in these days and times the choice to limit voice chat to team only is probably for the best.

I had very high hopes for this game at the start, and I played through a LOT of terrible patches, dealt with a lot of terrible bugs, lag, etc. I accepted the aggressive monetization as well, hoping the devs would, at some point, figure this game out and start giving us BASIC multiplayer functionalities. We still don't have them.

The thing that's really biting me about this game is that even if you have a game without bugs or lag (rare, but possible) there's a very key thing missing. Matchmaking. I am so absolutely beyond tired of getting games where no matter how perfect my defense is, no matter how perfect my passes are, the game is lost because all my team wants to do is shoot for a goal no matter how terrible the shot. 

Normally, in a functioning game, this would just mean that they get stuck at low MMR while I would rise due to individual performance. That is not how this game works. It has not gotten better even remotely since launch, and due to this as well as many, many other problems outlined in other negative reviews, the player count is very low, making even matches even less likely than before. Even in "Ranked" modes, this is the case, because the Ranks are functionally meaningless. You're in Gold, you'll get matched with low Bronze and lose MMR because *they* don't know how to play. 
You get no bonuses for individual performance or anything. The game doesn't care if you have 100 passes and your teammates have 0. -8 points, eat s**t.

The game also has an incredible toxicity issue with players constantly leaving after they give up a free goal, or just griefing the game and self-scoring because things didn't go their way. This happens at least once every 3 games. They added a report button hidden behind 2 screens, and it does absolutely nothing whatsoever. 

To recap: 
40$ game at launch. We are now 7 months in.
Still no actual matchmaking. Still no actual report button. Still no actual ranked mode. Bugs from launch are still there. New bugs have since been added to the game. Extremely prevalent lag issues and often low network stability.
But at least we have two Balenciaga collaborations!

Stay away from this game.

Frequent crashes.
Let alone the ball randomly teleporting, not appearing and other desync/networking issues which are WAY too big.

List can go on but I think the developers nowadays just went crazy. You can't ask me $35 for this game which is literally in an unplayable state right now and even have the courage to say it's a "Full Release".

[b]REMATCH – Quick Take[/b]

REMATCH, from the team behind Sifu, offers a fresh, high-octane twist on soccer: 5v5 matches, no fouls or offsides, and tight, fast-paced arenas from a third-person POV.

[b]Pros:[/b]
✔️ Intuitive yet deep gameplay
✔️ Colorful, stylized visuals
✔️ Smooth performance – even on Steam Deck

[b]Cons:[/b]
❌ Some desync and stability issues – occasional crashes and bugs
❌ Matchmaking can lag if teammates aren’t cooperating

[b]Storage:[/b]
Despite Steam listing a [b]20GB[/b] requirement, the actual install size is only [b]6.31GB[/b] – so don’t worry about burning space!

[b]Verdict:[/b]
Fast, stylish, and surprisingly fun – even if you're not a traditional soccer fan. Worth jumping into, especially since it’s light on storage and runs great.

Genuinely the most fun I've had in awhile. I've never really enjoyed FIFA games, but I have a boat load of hours in rocket league tho, so coming to this, is a breathe of fresh air.

"A chaotic, competitive soccer brawler that somehow got me into soccer. I'm shocked too."

Rematch is a fast-paced 5v5 soccer game where positions change on the fly—you're the goalie, then you're the striker, then you're just trying to survive. It's super easy to pick up, and once it clicks, it clicks. Scoring feels amazing, dribbling and goalkeeping are surprisingly smooth, and the action is nonstop. Matches are only 6 minutes, so it's a great pick-up-and-play kind of game that can casually eat hours of your life.

Best played with friends. Whether it's 3v3, 4v4, or full-on 5v5, the chaos becomes way more fun with a squad. Teammates can be your best friend or your worst nightmare—there is no in-between. Playing solo can be frustrating, especially when your team doesn’t pass, but you only need to survive 6 minutes. You'll live.

The graphics are clean and futuristic, the UI’s decent (though it could use some polish), and overall the game feels good—when it works. And that's where things start to unravel.

The ball will pass through goalkeepers even while the save animation plays.

Ball desync is wild, especially off goalposts.

Rubber-banding is common.

High server latency and syncing issues are frequent, especially with global players.

There's no push-to-talk in voice chat—just always-on or mute.

Bugs like giving the ball to the enemy after they score? Yeah, those exist.

I’ve also had a few random crashes and moments that made me question if someone just lagged out or snapped reality in half.

Honestly? The beta felt smoother. I had 8.1 hours in the beta, now 5.9 in advanced access—and it feels like a step back, not forward. There's still fun to be had, but it’s held back by a ton of technical issues. If those get fixed, this could easily become a top-tier indie multiplayer title.

For the current price? It’s okay. But $15–$20 would feel more fair, especially with how buggy the release has been.

That said… the fun factor is a 10/10. This is the kind of game that hooks you when you're with at least one friend. You’ll yell, laugh, and suddenly realize you’ve been playing for 3 hours straight. I don't even like soccer like that, but Rematch made me care. That’s worth something.

Would I recommend it?
If you can handle jank, rubber-banding, and the occasional "why did the ball just vanish?" moment—yes.
But if you’re hoping for polish? Maybe wait for a few more patches.
Still, I’ll probably be in queue anyway… yelling at the ball.

Recomiendo el juego, es un buen juego competitivo y entre amigos es genial, pero hay que tener varias cosas en cuenta.

En el estado actual del juego hay demasiados errores y cosas que están funcionando mal, sobre todo los servidores, no evita que el juego sea jugable, pero en algunas ocasiones particulares es frustrante.
Por estos motivos el juego está recibiendo reseñas negativas y es normal, pero ya es costumbre que en los juegos competitivos de hoy en día siempre salgan con errores o poco contenido, como fue el caso de Rocket League hace muchos años o CS2, un caso relativamente actual.
Mi sincera opinión es que todos estos problemas se irán solucionando con el tiempo, no hacen a la experiencia injugable, pero si te hace dudar a la hora de entrar a una clasificatoria, si querés una experiencia pulida diría que esperes antes de comprar, un par de meses en lo que cada cosa se pone en su lugar, si esto no te molesta y simplemente querés pasarla bien podes comprarlo ahora mismo.

También es imposible asegurar el futuro del juego ya que depende que tanto rango de "habilidad" pueda tener a futuro, actualmente hay un par de "trucos/técnicas" que no son las básicas del juego, que son divertidas, que toman un poco de tiempo en aprenderse y hacen que el gameplay sea más entretenido, pero si a futuro no se genera una curva de aprendizaje más marcada es evidente que el juego se va a estancar. Por la otra parte hablando del contenido se ve a simple vista que van a bombarder a ítems de personalización así que eso no sería un problema.

En resumen hoy en día con como estamos no puedo asegurar el futuro del juego, pero si está claro que estos errores generales como ver gente teletransportandose, quitar un balón y que se le devuelva al rival, o atajar una pelota y aun así se marque gol por dar unos ejemplos, se van a solucionar más temprano que tarde así que no lo tendría demasiado en cuenta, para determinar el futuro hay que ver como los desarrolladores interactuan con la comunidad para mejorar y complejizar el gameplay.

19/06/25 Edit: Acaba de salir el juego oficialmente y por mi parte con las hotfix el juego ha corregido muchos errores y aunque siguen habiendo no es comparable al early acces, esto en teoría debería seguir mejorando así que es una muy buena señal.

También he leído de ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ que los items se compran con dinero real y que no esperaban eso de un juego pago (no puedo creer la nula capacidad motriz que tienen), pero la verdad es que el juego no tiene ese problema ya que hay 2 tipos de monedas, una de paga y otra que te dan simplemente jugando, rinde más simplemente jugar 30 minutos para conseguir las monedas normales, que meter dinero para comprarse un accesorio, la única razón para cargar ese tipo de monedas sería para comprar el pase o comprar un personaje como es el actual caso con Ronaldinho. En resumen siento que son más valuables las monedas que te dan por jugar que las que se compran. Lujos de ser un juego de paga.

TLDR: Some minor server flaws and gameplay bugs (especially on mouse and keyboard) but it's so incredibly addictive win or lose.

I recommend this game, but only if you like games for their concept, not their execution.

Right now, the servers are a mess — constant lag in a game that’s trying to be competitive is just unacceptable. They seriously need to fix that, to the point where it it feels unplayable at times :(

That said… the gameplay? It slaps. It gives me that same curious spark I felt when Rocket League dropped — it’s unique, it’s fun, and it has real potential as a new esport. I’m genuinely excited to see where it goes.

I went all-in and bought the Elite edition (30 bucks more) just to play early. And honestly? Unless they start adding actual content (not just cosmetics), this version is not worth that extra money. The experience feels way too mid for that price.

Still, I believe in this game. It’s way too cool to flop. I’m hoping it gets better — because it deserves to.

I want to love this game but ever since this last beta the game has been so unstable. Hard crashes (with no rejoin option), significant animation issues, delay on the ball and other players. You can't have a competitive game where the ball constantly jitters and outcomes are random depending on current latency when attempting to make plays. You start thinking, "Is my opponent's connection stable enough to do normal timing or do I have to do this early/late?" 

The game is fantastic when it works correctly but after having multiple balls phasing through me as goalie, weird interactions (intercepting the ball with a ground intercept while its above my head mid-butterfly), intercepting and striking while 5+ feet away from the ball, and all the janky ball behavior in general, I'd say this needs to cook longer. And all this is considering I mostly play 3v3. 5v5 (the only ranked mode, btw) was borderline unplayable in the last beta and I'd wager its the same here. I'll come back to it eventually but its disappointing how it launched this way.
It's fun but it's really hard when the diorection movement is based on mouse. Not keyboard. Really not used to that and i play a lot of games.

I'm only giving this game a thumbs up because the game itself is incredibly fun. The netcode/server issues make at least 50% of matches unplayable currently. I have high hopes that they fix these issues as they have a golden game if they do. Otherwise, no one is gonna play this game moving forward if these continue to be an issue. Very hopeful, and very disappointing that this is the state of the game for those of us that paid for pre-release.

I really love this game and so badly want to recommend it, but it's hardly improved since the beta and in a really bad place right now. 

I've played a fair amount of games now and am yet to play one without multiple people crashing/disconnecting. The fact that you can't reconnect to non-ranked games makes playing with friends pretty frustrating. 

I really hope they fix this and that I can change this review to positive, but not being able to play a single game in its entirety with the same lobby of people is a huge negative.

EDIT:
The crashes have finally been fixed and I can easily recommend this game now. There's still a handful of issues like desync, ball teleportation, and the occasional crash, but by and large the game is running very consistently well. There are definitely still a few kinks to iron out, but the overall package is definitely worthy of a recommendation.

Sure you might not be flying through the sky like in Rocket League... But the mechanics are pretty solid and lend themselves to a pretty high skill ceiling.

Others have mentioned the netcode and lag being a bit of an issue and I will agree. I will refrain from playing ranked matches until it becomes more stable. I do hope that they introduce a solo mode with bots so that you can work on some of the hidden mechanics without looking like a fool in an online match. Either that, or add an optional more advanced tutorial after completing the basics.

Game is very good, Here is what I like:

1) Getting into the game: You can know nothing about soccer/football and be able to enjoy this. The general premise is there, it just takes away all the penalties and annoying OOB rules, leaving something that you can keep going with till someone scores. Great stuff.

2) Depth: Game gives you a decent amount of tools to control, and take control of the ball and move yourself around. learning when and how to use those tools is what makes this game so much fun to keep playing, and there is tons to figure out.

3) The Multiplayer: For the mostpart, playing online has been terrific. Games as of the last few patches has been quicker to queue into. Games have played smoothly for the most part, and ranked is a thing you can play if you prefer playing more competitively.

And the stuff I don't like:

1) Lack of Single player.... There are little to no single player elements in this game. It seems like there will be some sort of story mode, but it feels like its being drip fed, currently its like 3 tutorial missions with some rather nice cut scenes in between. from what I have seen, single player just comprises of practice, the freeplay mode (empty field by hitting escape), and the prologue right now. 

2) Bugs... [Note: alot of these have been fixed, game has not crashed for me in a while, possession bug I have not encountered anymore.] I have had a few times now the ball starts in the possession of a random player? rather than the center guy passing it back? Game is crashing sometimes during celebration scenes? Thought these were ironed out after the beta, hopefully they get fixed soon.

3) Lag? the responsiveness can feel a little jank at times, where I intercept the ball, get feedback for shin kicking it from an opponent, and said opponent would just keep the ball like nothing happened. this might just be more inline with a bug.

4) THE SEASONAL GRIND...... I would like more daily missions, I presume this will be something added later on, but just "Win one game" is kinda lame. I originally had an issue with the amount of progress you got to earn season points, however regularly playing 5s seems to net me at least 1000 on average playing decently well. so not that bad.

5) THE STORE... I have mixed feelings being asked to spend more money after I paid like 60$ to get this game. You can earn (for the most part everything) by playing regularly and earning points to buy cosmetics, however there are still some locked behind that paywall. You also need to spend 10$ to get the Season pass, or you need to burn a captian pass for access, which... also... I have mixed feelings about.

6) The multiplayer..... Some games (seldom albeit) have been super laggy, to the point where the ball literally teleports out of my posession? Also I have gotten games where people just spout the most annoying unhelpful ♥♥♥♥ over mic (which lets face it, is bog standard for any multiplayer game), but after a few games where coms are for the most part annoying as ♥♥♥♥, I've just turned voice off. cudos to the people who actually give constructive communication. Though, some form of reporting system would be greatly appreciated for people who are either trolling, reeeee-ing, or passively aggressively spamming 'Thanks' every time we get scored on.

7) No replays? I hope this will be a feature later on in this games lifespan. I get some great games every now and again that I would love to review to improve.

Aside from those issues, this game is terrific. I have enjoyed quite a bit of the beta, and when I have more free time, I will be sinking in alot more time into this game. Currently an 8.5/10. Has potential to be the best soccer / soccer-like game ever. Sloclap delivers once again.

This game could be huge if it touched the right player base...

Rome wasn't built in a day is the best way to describe what this game is trying to accomplish. 

You've got your server issues. Of course it's going to be better in the closed beta but it's better than when it was in the open beta as well. They have to stress test this stuff. I get it, everyone expects more but I just can't bring myself to not enjoy the game. If you've been gaming as long as I have, then you understand that they will get it done. If we as a fanbase don't support them (that doesn't necessarily mean with wallets) then no one will. TL;DR - Support the devs by being encouraging and just wait to play the game. 

Gameplay:
The servers aren't great, I streamed for 16 hours and have evidence of the ball being caught as GK and the replay showing I either didn't move or the ball goes through my arms. Granted, I did only crash once but that wasn't until 12hrs into my session and I streamed for a total of 16hrs. 
Lots of inconsistencies in terms of gameplay overall.   So take that as you will. The opposing team will have possession of the ball randomly, even though they just scored. The GK skills not taking priority when tackling or just ball handling in general for GK. People like to blame GK but without a solid defense working with you to open up the field, it's near impossible to make some decent saves on your own with the bugs in the GK box. 

Defense is ok (more so to do with tm8s positioning) I suppose, the dash chop while in defensive stance is your best friend for sure. That and defensive stance directional dash. It allows you to stay with your opponent without lunging, in case they get dribble happy. It's saved me a few times. Positioning is key if your team doesn't try to bomb rush the baller. 

Offense has a lot of abilities for you to play around with. I know I've barely scratched the surface with what you can do while utilizing effort. The controls are rather simple and easy to pick up and play. This game definitely lends itself to a casual experience in that regard. Nothing like Rocket League but that's to be expected. I'm sure further updates could remedy this in the future. The ball magnetism has properties that illude me and other players. It wants to stick to your feet in the worst situations but on wide open passes while in the lane for the pass, it'll just whiz right by you. Performing action on the blue indicator will be your way of getting the timing down for cleaner passes and shots but determining priority during 50-50s is hard. I feel like I beat people and then my character just gets megged. Also, the stale ball, when it just stays in place is awkward when it's clear you or the opponent had more momentum behind their movement. Momentum doesn't seem to be taken into account as much as just being to the ball first. I know you're not meant to hold the ball long but I'd at least like a chance to pass it.

Main modes: 4s is probably the most balanced with skilled players and ball chasers. Everyone wants to ego push or spam the call button when being trapped by defenders in 3s. In the intended gamemode, 5s, It's ok. A lot of players on the field. If your opponents are smart, they'll let you cluster and cherrypick if you allow them outside of your formations. The game is still early and I expect the use of radar to really shake things up once people gather the vision. My favorite mode is 4s due to how balanced it is for solos. 5s almost always lands me with a team that's willing to blame me for everything. 3s, people just quit when they don't get their way. It is what it is. 
The training packs are a fun way of taking a break from the overall online aspect of the game as well. Smoother and a much better way of getting good as they say. Variety of modes and always the ability to do better than the prior attempt. It really just needs a retry mode. I shouldn't have to go to the menu every  time I want to do multiple sessions of the same mode. On that note. I get not rematching in ranked but casual needs a queue to rematch in a game of the same name. Come on now. I



Customization: 
I didn't spend too much time customizing. I didn't mess around with the tier items at all since I did most of that in the beta. It's about the same as any other battle pass except you have to earn the tokens and spend them to gain towards whichever line of items you want before continuing into the next season and doing it all over again. The ability to choose the line of items you find most appealing is nice. Cuts down on the amount of useless gear or items in your stash for sure. 
Characters are robust with scaling of facial features while utilizing presets. Plenty of skin tone options and even vitiligo. All your other basic hair, make up, shades of hair and highlights. It gets rather in depth and I could see people spending up to 30 minutes on just setting up a preset to look good for the camera.  It's honestly a lot more in depth than I thought it would be for this game. I just expected a bunch of preset characters, so it's a welcome addition.
Equipment is layered and you can you mix and match to your hearts content. Color options are plentiful, my only gripe is that black on the shoes in game is showing as purple. Everything else equipment wise is showing as the normal shade. All pieces of equipment can be modified in terms of color. Jerseys can change patterns, as well as pants / shorts and under garments. Again, this gets pretty in depth, I don't intend on spending too much time on it. You're better off experiencing it for yourself. You can have multiple presets. Have fun. 


TL;DR - If you don't want to be apart of the trend of a game getting better over time, no harm no foul. You're not going to miss much. I know for a lot of people, crossplay is an issue. It's not for me so I don't care much. Just wait a year and play it when it's "Finished". To which I hope it never is and continues to evolve. I for one am here to support a unique project and urge sloclap to ignore the haters and just keep doing their thing. 


After all of the above, I still recommend the game. After playing for 16hrs from time of release on steam,  I can confidently say 
that I had a great time meeting potential teammates. Going for sick passing plays, getting hat tricks and saving the day as the GK. I think this game has a ton of potential and you should definitely waste your time on here if you enjoy team play or just a casual soccer game that you want to showboat on. It's a blast.

6/18 UPDATE: they fixed the ball possession at kick off issue, however there are still a few crashes, and it mostly seems to stem from the replays after games.

I played during Beta 1 and Beta 2 and I purchased the Elite Edition of the game. However, I cannot recommend the game in its current state as the developer is working through some network issues. There are some bugs that keep coming up that make the game frustrating to play. Some examples are: the ball teleporting directly to an opponent during a kick off, goalie pickup animations causing you to miss the ball, and the ball getting stuck in place when multiple players have collision with it. I think that the idea is fantastic and I have had a lot of fun playing with my friends. However, I think $30 is a bit much for the content in the game currently (3v3, 4v4, and 5v5 ranked and unranked) and especially with the bugs and network issues in the game currently. While the developer has been pretty transparent with their posts and now the inclusion of patch notes, I wonder if they have been held to a deadline by their publisher which stopped them from being able to delay the game to work on the current issues in the game.

Je vais parler de mon expérience en 2 temps, à savoir avant la sortie du jeu, puis après. Lisez jusqu’à la fin parce que ma note est nuancée...

OPEN BETA
J'ai pu jouer aux deux premières Open Beta de Rematch. J'ai dû lâcher pas moins de 30h de jeu. Concernant le Gameplay, c'est facile à prendre en main au début. Les mécaniques sont basiques mais diablement efficaces.

Néanmoins, n'allez pas croire que c'est un jeu simple. La difficulté pointe le bout de son nez quand le travail d'équipe intervient. Jouer avec des teammates avec un minimum d'expérience de jeu permet de créer des compositions d'attaques mêlant dribbles, passes, lobs et tirs TRES satisfaisants et addictifs.

Pas mal de soucis cependant avec le netcode durant l'Open Beta (la balle est censée être dans mes pieds, elle finit gare du Nord). Aucun souci pour moi à ce moment, c'est une open beta, c'est fait pour test.

Par contre, on peut sentir l’énorme étron économique du jeu arriver. Encore un jeu qui va sortir payant à la sortie, et qui proposera un MAGNIFIQUE "pass de combat" en plus. Néanmoins, plusieurs items sont achetables avec de la monnaie obtenue après les parties. C'est pas mal une fois qu'on s'est chié dessus et qu'on doit serrer des fesses.

SORTIE DU JEU
Je suis un pigeon qui aime gaspiller son argent alors j'ai acheté le jeu en Pro Edition. (de plus j'ai confiance au studio depuis le magnifique SIFU)

Résultat :

Netcode toujours présent

Plusieurs nouveaux bugs sympathiques comme un TAB pouvant comporter jusqu'à 15 joueurs, ou des crashs de session (tous ces problèmes seraient pardonnés si le jeu était un FTP)

Un système économique DE GROS CHIENS QUI VEULENT DES SOUS alors que le jeu est PAYANT

Le jeu avait selon moi sa place dans la catégorie Free to Play, mais on ne sait pas pourquoi, c'est pas le cas.

J'aime le gameplay de Rematch, mais putain ça fait vraiment mal au cul de voir certain studio (ou éditeur) faire allègrement caca sur le porte monnaies des joueurs.

-Ranked in all game modes (3v3, 4v4, and 5v5, different ranks in each).

First day of release, absolutely cracking of a game so far, met some really lovely people to play with and just overall having a blast, more than any other game in recent memory, especially a football game

Want to preface this by saying that the foundation of the game is great, probably one of the better games released in recent times. If you've played Rocket League before, you're effectively playing that game if they decided to replace the cars with people and added a Football flair to it. At its core its one of the better multiplayer games out on the market right now from a purely gameplay perspective.

That being said, the reason why its difficult to recommend is that it seems like too much of a focus was put there and not anywhere else in the game, leading to having too significant of your time being frustrated with the faulty technical aspects. The big issues I have with the game include, but are not limited to:

- No report system

- If playing on Keyboard and Mouse (which after trying both this and controller feels like the superior control scheme), the game currently offers no way to aim in a direction with WASD. What this means that if you want to play a through ball, you'll need to press the tap pass button AND move your mouse in that direction. When the chaos is far more controlled, this isn't a huge deal and easy to work with, but in higher-pressure scenarios this leads to plenty of mistakes where the ball is not going where you were wanting it to. I've had this happen not only with myself, but have seen numerous teammates where I can assume they were going for a cross using the lob pass (which uses the same aiming scheme), only to ship the ball back towards midfield.

- EAC being apart of the game, but I have just left a game with a speedhacker, so EAC once again, despite being everywhere, does nothing to divert cheats and sits to just farm data at a kernel-level. I understand its a cat & mouse race to catch cheats, but what is the point of this product being given this level of access to my machine and it has the same effectiveness as VAC, but with more issues associated with it?

- Server ping instability - extremely common to get varying pings on us-east (my main living location) stretching from 30ms to 90ms. Even when trying us-central for a while the same issues occur, albeit I can say that us-central offered me more stable games from a ping perspective despite getting servers that can go from 40ms-100ms.

- If you're pinging fine to the server but are against someone who is pinging 90ms, the game suffers from awful desync, making it far more difficult than it needs to to read an opponents movement on the ball as they end up almost teleporting on your screen.

- While this point has noticeably become better since launch, if you're playing GK you can suffer instances still where you character goes into a saving animation for the ball and doesn't save or, due to the desync issue I mentioned, can have the ball saved on your screen and it go right by you.

- Ranked mode currently doesn't offer any level of matchmaking. Calling this "Season 0" isn't an excuse to ship this game advertised as a full product with Ranked play, but lack any matchmaking. What I mean by this is that the game is reported to have

- Despite the game telling you that there is a penalty for leaving games early, this is not the case. I've tested a session where I left all my games with close to no time left on the clock for about an hour of in-game time and have yet to receive any sort of penalty for doing so, so imagine if word on that games out widespread and everyone finds out that you can do this freely?
-- On that note, when someone leaves your game, most of the time the game is pretty good about finding a replacement if it is early on, but I've had numerous instances that once the game goes past the halfway mark (and sometimes even earlier) and someone leaves, then the game will not find a replacement. Guess its time to leave that game and queue for another without penalty for doing so!

- I put this one towards the bottom as this is always going to be an issue with team-based games, but Rematch is quite egregious about it. This is one of those multiplayer games where it feels that if you don't have a consistent group to play with, you end up rolling dice on the quality of teammates, except the dice are weighted. The best multiplayer games I've played have been the ones where I don't feel like I need to message my friends to get on and play as I have enough agency on my own. While I can say that there is more of that solo agency in this game, its not quite enough as at the end of the day there is only one ball on the pitch.

The biggest spit in the face about all of this is that plenty of these have been reported in the betas, during advanced access, and since the launch of the game and only some of the issues have become better. There have been numerous patches now where the devs had stated that server stability, queue times, desync issues have been fixed/improved, but it feels the exact same as it did before. Of course, microtransactions are still being sold and cosmetics are being put into the game without issues, but we can't fix the issues that we keep claiming are fixed in each patch? We can also get Ronaldinho to come visit before the games launch, but can't put that time and effort into ensuring these technical aspects are functioning before we sell this to players?

Again, from a purely gameplay perspective, this game is awesome. I can only imagine how much fun it would be to get some friends at a LAN Party (remember those?) and to play a game like this; its honestly the perfect formula and Rocket League showed us over a decade ago that we want a game like this with the same online functionalities, but it'd be nice to get a functional experience without these issues that should have been addressed before launch. I'm still optimistic that in due time this game will become better and I can rewrite a positive review for this game, but for now I'd highly recommend putting that $30 elsewhere.

这个游戏很好玩，这个评价最终应该会改成是好评，写差评是希望制作方能改进。
第一天怒玩10小时，觉得这游戏有一些问题需要改一下

1.网络问题。对于中国大陆玩家非常不友好，开了加速器也会出现多次游戏中掉线失去连接的情况。希望有大陆服务器，现在中国大陆玩家的Steam付费玩家很多，剑星和黑神话之类的游戏已经证明了中国玩家的购买力，希望制作组予以重视中国市场。
2.BUG问题。经常本该是自家的发球回合，但是球却随机出现在对方脚下开球，非常不友好
窗口化界面的时候有时候会出现分辨率突然降低导致窗口缩小的情况
3.操作问题。右键将鼠标类似遥感拖动来锁方向我认为不是好的设计，在实战中按照自己心意运用有些困难，而且失误造成的代价非常大（一个向后的误传会直接形成对面反击），希望是右键+wasd加上两两组合实现八个方向的快速传球就足够满足绝大部分时候得需要。
4.外挂问题。第一天已经出现了使用无限加速功能的外挂的作弊玩家，希望官方予以重视。
5.功能单薄。希望推出俱乐部功能让一起玩的玩家能共同建设，以后还可以举办俱乐杯赛之类的活动。

游戏是好游戏，真的非常有乐趣，但现在问题还是很多，希望制作方改进。

I initially left a positive review for this game, it was a great game to retire Rocket League for however all things come with a catch after all.

This game has been out for nearly 2 months and we still have the same issues we saw initially on launch, biggest of which being the netcode of this game.

This game was not ready for release, the beta tests before that was a pure telltale sign of that and charging 30 dollars for this game I will admit is a bit steep, and I have a well paying j*b before anyone says "j*bless". I'm a firm believer that this game should not be Free to Play however maybe $15-20 would've made general reception much better.

This being said, you do pay $30 to own and play the game and where is all that money going? Surely not the servers that EVERYONE has complained about since launch right? Nahhh that'd make too much sense.

The ranked mode and how it works is a complete joke. First of which, you're not matched with your own bracket. Its completely random who you go against. You can be a Silver player going against a 5 stack of Masters and vice versa. You cannot derank down at all. You can go down divisions but never your rank in general which allows people to throw matches much easier if they don't think their team is going to do anything when they're already at 0RP in their rank, they have nothing to lose AT ALL.

The desync issues persist, the community is getting worse with a lot of Rocket League toxicity seeping through and its sad to see. I hope these issues get righted because I do love this form of soccer game more than any Fifa top down boring BS and I can see this game being very competitive and fun if they FIX THE ISSUES.

It's here, and it's all here. 

I first saw this game at the game awards last year, trailer after trailer, bored out of my mind, and then a colorful soccer game by of all studios Sloclap, the developers of Sifu, caught my eye. 

Rematch is a genuinely uniquely fun game, before I played the beta I had no knowledge of soccer or interest in it. Today I played seven straight hours and had a blast. There are some issues with the networking, but it's nothing that can't be fixed with netcode updates in the future. I've heard of some issues with crashing, but I haven't had any yet, though that's not to say that it isn't a common problem, just that I can't speak to it.

Rematch is truly special because it makes me feel amped, there's a feeling of actual material excitement with every goal, irrelevant of if it was you scoring it. It's the first game I've ever genuinely been stunned by my team's successes, my own successes, and the enemy team's successes. The core gameplay is simple to learn but very difficult to master and make flow easily in your head. 

The game has some issues, some major and some minor, but the foundations this game is built on are very, VERY strong. If you're hesitant, wait a few weeks to a month and I'd wager Sloclap will have most of the major netcode issues ironed out. If you don't mind chugging along, helping out with the growing pains, then please please pick this up. I hope I run into you on the pitch :)

Rating: 2/5 ( I still highly recommend the game — the core gameplay is fantastic. But at this point, it's entirely up to the developers to step up and address these issues. )

A game full of potential, completely ruined by the lack of basic competitive features

This game could be great. It should be great. But right now, it’s being held back by the complete absence of fundamental systems that are standard in any modern competitive title.

First of all – where is the report system? The game is flooded with players who intentionally ruin matches. There’s absolutely no way to punish or report them, and it’s making the experience unbearable.

Second – why are we still punished with full -16 point losses when a teammate disconnects or rage-quits early on? Playing 4v5 or even 3v5 and losing as a result shouldn't lead to the same punishment as losing a fair match. It’s lazy game design and punishes players for things outside their control.

And finally, let’s talk about solo players. If you’re not playing with a full coordinated team, your performance means absolutely nothing. You can top the scoreboard, carry your team, play nearly perfectly – and still lose because the matchmaking throws you into a team of clueless players.
There is ZERO skill-based adjustment in the ranking system.
Any decent game with an ELO or MMR system takes individual performance into account. This game doesn’t. It’s outdated, unfair, and infuriating.

Summary:
If the devs don’t add a proper report system, rage-quit protection, and performance-based ranking, this game will stay stuck in mediocrity, no matter how fun the core gameplay is.

At this point, I don't know if I would recommend it. The game is great, fun too. Gameplay is easy to learn, hard to master. It all comes together... but...
A lot of bugs, poor servers and gameplay synchronization.

Sometimes more frustrating than fun.

A couple of examples:
- after joining a match you can't move
- after losing a goal and starting the game from the middle, the ball is not in the middle of the field but already at the feet of the opponent, who is waiting until our animation "passing the ball from the middle is over" and can already run with a counter attack
- synchronization of the reception of the ball - it seems to you that you have received the ball but it flies through you or even the icon surrounding the ball to receive it doesn't appear and you can't do anything about it, you can only start running after it and hope to take control of it

In addition, achievements that are obtainable in the game do not count at all. I won't count how many times I've assisted on a goal as a goalkeeper, I won't count how many ranking matches I've won, or how many times I've managed to win 3 matches in a row (whether casual or ranking)

It is a bit pathetic that even such a simple thing is broken

Unfortunately, but in both of the last two beta tests there were no such problems as I experienced now. (At least I didn't run into such bugs)

Everything is fixable and this game can achieve the success of Rocket League, I'm keeping my fingers crossed for that, but now it still needs polishing.

I would also like to add that the system for selecting positions at the start of the match is a joke. I started the match as a goalkeeper eight times in a row, and I had four or five situations where I practically started the game as a goalkeeper all the time. Even restarting the game didn't help. Then you end up in a match where you stand in goal the whole game because no one has scored a goal, and the match lasts six minutes plus another ten minutes of added time.
I know it depends on luck, but isn't there some kind of system? 
"This player plays as a goalkeeper very often, let's put him in the field"

Not to mention players who are goalkeepers scoring own goals because they don't want to stand in goal. They assume that “I'll score a goal soon too".  So either they leave the goal and the penalty area is empty because no one wants to replace them, or they score own goals to change faster.

It's taking longer and longer to find a match, we need a reporting system, the promised crossplay and better netcode.

Loco, no les voy a mentir: Rematch es más picante que una final de Libertadores en la Bombonera con lluvia, penales y VAR en el minuto 96.

Arranqué jugando con el San Lorenzo de los ‘90 (porque soy un nostálgico y masoquista) y terminé puteando como el Cholo Simeone en un clásico. El nivel de detalle es tan enfermizo que hasta mi vieja, que odia el fútbol, se quedó mirando un partido porque pensó que era de verdad. Me gritó: "¡Apagá la tele y poné la mesa!"... ¡pero si es la compu, vieja!

⚽ Puntos fuertes:

    La IA te mete más presión que el River de Gallardo en su mejor época.

    Los goles se gritan de verdad. A uno lo festejé tanto que desperté al nene y se puso a llorar... de la emoción (o del susto, no sé).

    Tiene más táctica que el almacenero de la esquina que fía sin interés.

⚠️ Puntos flacos:

    No podés insultar al árbitro directamente. Una falta grave, loco. ¡Queremos botón de puteada YA!

    Cuando tu jugador estrella se lesiona solo por correr... ahí te das cuenta que es más realista que la vida misma.

    Mi novia me dejó después de 30 horas seguidas en “modo campaña”... pero gané la Champions, así que valió la pena.

💥 En resumen, si sos futbolero y te gusta putear, emocionarte, tirar tácticas imposibles y festejar como si hubieras ganado el Mundial con gol de taco, Rematch es tu droga digital. Eso sí, preparate café, calmantes y tal vez un abogado si despertás a todo el edificio a los gritos.
10/10. Le pondría 11 si pudiera, como al Diego.

Let me preface this with: I played a LOT of Rocket League and might unfairly compare these games. 

The game is basically soccer with a little bit finer control of movement and the ball. Keyboard and Mouse don't feel right and without an automatic cam, my fingers are a constant mess between switching buttons and stick. Not a fan, 2 Games made my hand cramp. 

The controls feel "mushy" - the player character has a ton of inertia which makes fast turns extremely awkward. Depending on your skill level and game mode it feel like half the game is done standing around, while hoping for a teammate to find the passing button. 

Speaking of buttons: It's one of the reasons why i adore rocket league, there is no "aim" mechanic. Sure, this game doesn't promise that , but in the end it just doesn't really feel good to "press a button to score". 

The gameplay goes from "slow and pretty much not moving " to "so fast you don't really have a chance to do anything". A lower reaction player probably will even have trouble keeping track of the ball - let alone any involvement in any games.

[h3]Positives[/h3]
[*]Charakter Editor 
[*]Battlepass
[*]Torjubel
[*]Arenen 
[*]Sieges-Posen nach Match
[*]Teilweise spannende Spielpartien 
[*]Updates die das Spiel verbessert haben

[h3]Negatives[/h3]
Es gibt noch einige kleinere Bugs und technische Fehler, die den Spielfluss beeinträchtigen, hoffe das diese schnell behoben werden..

[*]Bälle, die durch einen hindurchgehen.
[*]Grätschen, die durch den Ball hindurchgehen.
[*]Teilweise etwas RNG-lastig, zumindest mein Eindruck.
[*]Flachpässe verziehen oft (Rechte Maustaste) und (F)-Taste
[*]Das Rangsystem fühlt sich für mich an, als würde man sich kontinuierlich im Kreis drehen. Außerdem bietet das Spiel für den Zeitaufwand viel zu wenig Fortschritt. 

Der Rangmodus frustriert, der Battlepass kompensiert das nur kurzfristig.

[h3]Fazit:[/h3]
Die Spielpartien sind spannungsgeladen, gleichzeitig jedoch auch stressig und zeitweise total langweilig. Es kommt nicht selten vor das nach zwei Runden die Luft raus ist. Der Rangmodus ist verbesserungswürdig, da Bemühungen kaum anerkannt werden und das ständige Auf- und Absteigen kein Fortschritt bringt. Mit Tägliche Herausforderungen, Events, Levelbelohnungen und Quests könnte man dem entgegenwirken. Unterm Strich zeigt das Spiel viel Potenzial, an Balance und Feintuning sollte jedoch noch gearbeitet werden.

Как аркадный футбол, который жутко упрощён  очень даже хорош! Но за 1500 касаря+ такое себе. Если ли бы не друг скинул гифтом точно бы не купил! + Гемплей, настройки персонажей, полей и другой косметики! + также есть голосовой чат очень помогает если играете соло( но в рейтинг играть соло это смерть! Можете готовится что за всю игру, не считая отборов вы троните мяч или поучаствуйте в атаке 2-3 раза! Для команды в таком случае вы просто машина по подаче мячей для их игры. Так-что без компании играйте на свой трах и риск! Но это не критично в любом случае! такое будет происходить 2-3 играх из 10 ) НУ а главный минус- это сервера или коннект или оптимизация хотя фпс не прыгает от слова совсем! Мяч может телепортироваться  как сумасшедший по сему полю! порой мяч может быть где угодно но только не так где вы ожидаете и не там где его показывает игра! играя за вратаря это самая большая проблема! Из а таких проблем с серверами вся игра убивается за секунды. Но думаю если вы живете через дорогу от сервера то таких проблем у вас быть не может. Вообщем леди и джентльмены игре есть куда расти и есть к чему стремиться! Пока сыровата и денег своих думаю не стоит. Но если механическую часть игры думаю будет бодрый АРКАДНЫЙ футбол! Но тем не менее в эту игру советую поиграть!!!!

I really enjoyed the beta to the point I bought the pro edition.

Yet after playing it for a while I really couldn't see why this game couldn't stay in open beta or opt for free to play.

They've failed on cross-platform and not just consoles mind you but also steam to game pass players!

Next - they couldn't get that right but somehow could add Ronaldinho for 8£ which seems cheap but when you play the game you'll see the glaring issues.

Firstly - servers - I thought EA were bad for the big company they are but a team based game with up to 5 each game having this amount of packet loss or latency issues should be criminal.

One glitch which I saw a lot of was when your team concedes, you get kick off yet the ball disappears into the enemy teams hands somehow.

I thought it was just me until I heard the people in voice chat shouting "what the heck".

All in all, this game shouldn't be a paid product and not with the fact they've released season passes and a famous footballer as a skin from the get-go.

Also, very disappointed with the 3 day early access being one of the reasons to buy a pro edition as it's an EA tactic while slocap seem to call themselves a small company they still present themselves as a greedy company.

Very poor decision making and releasing the game way too early.

El fútbol es el deporte más lindo y más sano del mundo. De eso que no le quepa la menor duda a nadie. Porque se equivoque uno, no tiene que pagar el fútbol. Yo me equivoqué y pagué, pero la pelota no se mancha.

Vamos a ser rápidos.

· Puntos Negativos:

- Quizás a la larga, las mecánicas que son simples se pueden hacer repetitivas, pero no es algo nuevo o no lo consideraría del todo malo ya que juegos similares como Rocket League (aunque guardando sus distancias) terminan jugando a su favor este factor, sin embargo también es verdad que este juego es más de jugar en equipo y es clave para conseguir la victoria, si eres un chupón no vas a sacar nada.
 
- Quizás los servidores de vez en cuando dan tirones y el balón no va como debería, quizás en 1 de cada 5 partidos (me encuentro en España y estamos en acceso anticipado, sin embargo países como los de LATAM están teniendo problemas) aunque también es verdad que esto es un estudio indie y no tendrán aún suficientes servidores

· Puntos Positivos:

- Frescura en el género del fútbol, algo diferenciador con respecto a otros juegos y dando un enfoque cooperativo, en el que si no se trabaja en equipo puede jugar en tu contra (ya lo mencione con anterioridad)

- Facilidad para entender las mecánicas, pero difícil de dominar (recomendable jugar con mando, con teclado es algo tedioso)

- La personalización del personaje que aunque no es muy profundo es de agradecer la posibilidad de crear aberraciones o recrear jugadores reconocibles (aunque esto último poco durará con la llegada de más jugadores a parte de Ronaldinho, que ya se encuentra en el juego) Además de ello el estadio, efectos, etcétera.

- Futuras colaboraciones y la posibilidad que tiene este juego de hacer cosas llamativas si se lo proponen, porque potencial tiene.

- Apartado visual muy característico, el que haya jugado SIFU lo disfrutará mucho este apartado.

Recomendable la compra 100% si te gusta el fútbol.

Il calcio digitale ritrova la grinta!
Voto provvisorio: ⭐⭐⭐⭐☆ (4/5)

Dopo anni di dominio dei soliti noti, REMATCH arriva in accesso anticipato come una ventata d’aria fresca nel panorama dei giochi di calcio. E lo fa con grinta, stile e – soprattutto – un’anima tutta sua.

Il primo impatto è sorprendentemente solido: i controlli sono reattivi, il ritmo è dinamico e le partite scorrono con quel pizzico di imprevedibilità che rende ogni sfida diversa dalla precedente. L’impostazione arcade-simulativa trova un buon equilibrio, offrendo partite accessibili ma comunque tecniche per chi ama la profondità tattica.

Il comparto grafico, seppur non fotorealistico, è stilizzato con gusto e valorizza ogni azione in campo, mentre le animazioni – pur ancora perfettibili – restituiscono una sensazione di fisicità e impatto che mancava da troppo tempo nel genere. Il sistema di personalizzazione, già ora molto ricco, promette grandi cose in futuro.

Naturalmente qualche bug fa capolino: collisioni strane, qualche freeze sporadico e piccoli problemi generici. Ma nulla che rovini l’esperienza nel suo complesso, soprattutto considerando che gli sviluppatori sembrano ascoltare la community e aggiornano rapidamente.

In definitiva, REMATCH è un progetto ambizioso e, finalmente, genuinamente appassionato. Non è perfetto, ma è autentico. E dopo anni di cloni senz’anima, era proprio quello che serviva. Se amate il calcio e volete qualcosa di nuovo, dategli una chance: potrebbe sorprendervi.

Lors des BETAs les bugs sont tolérés sur tout les jeux, selon moi malheureusement maintenant le jeux est définitivement sorti, il s'agirait de régler ces bugs, et sa devrait déjà être fait au vu du prix du jeu. Des parties de Ranked ou le gardien ne peut plus attraper la balle, ou des joueurs ne peuvent plus courir avec le ballon mais uniquement tirer, des ballons qui dans aucun monde ne rentre dans le but mais qui compte quand même en ranked.

Le jeu est incroyable, le concept idem mais il faudrait que les développeurs commencent à se réveiller car je ne pense pas être le seul à être dégoûter de ces bugs de merde.

Un jeu sympa si vous jouez avec une équipe de pote. Sinon en soloQ c'est des appels dans le vent, beaucoup de personnes jouent trop solo balle au pied, le dernier défenseur qui dribble et perd le ballon (ou même le gardien), l'attaquant qui préfère tirer sur le gardien plutôt que de vous passer le ballon pour que vous marquiez (vous êtes démarqué mais la passe en retrait ne vient pas), les joueurs qui préfèrent jouer attaquant que défenseur, du coup les adversaires campent votre surface pour marquer sans pression, les joueurs qui quittent les cages car ils ne veulent pas être gardien.

Aussi, beaucoup de bugs persistent, le ballon donné aux adversaires alors qu'ils viennent de marquer, les tacles qui passent à travers le ballon, le ballon qui se fige et personne ne peut le bouger, le netcode à revoir car des joueurs se téléportent avec le ballon, des serveurs européens avec 12% de loss et ping à 150ms.

Pour conclure, la bombe : le cross-play non disponible sans aucune date prévue, annoncé une heure avant la sortie du jeu, pas terrible comme communication. Du coup les sessions de jeu avec les frères sur PS5 pas possible.

This game is fun! 

There is a lot of control over what you want to do as a player, unlike games like FIFA at times which have some frustrating controls.

Game lags sometimes and needs some network/server help. Although it can be frustrating at times, it didn't bother me very much, and I found that it doesn't detract from the overall fun the game provides. At least for someone like me who plans on playing casually.

I also have had zero issues playing on steam deck while my family is currently on vacation. Runs flawlessly.

Don't listen to the people complaining. Give the devs some time, and I trust the issues to be fixed. It's a smaller company taking on huge player base and server load.

If these cons aren't for you, thats totally fine, but more games which can be enjoyed casually need to be made.

Overall, super enjoyable game to play.

Awesome game, and a very unique concept.

It's a third person, skill-based competitive football game, made by the same people who made Sifu, and that part is important, because not only does it use the same very cool art style, but it also uses a very unique set of controls that at first are new to everyone, but they do that on purpose so you can master them.

In it's current state, it is very much worth the standard asking price for most people. 
The game modes available are very straight forward, Ranked play which is 5v5, Quickplay which is 5v5, 4v4 or 3v3, Custom(private) Matches, and the Prologue which is the tutorial for the game is very good and quick, but attaches itself to a very stylised story.

Network issues are unacceptably bad for a full release pre-ordered game. 

Even after selecting and playing in nearest region with <50ms ping there is constant rubber-banding, desync-teleporting, characters sliding around, ball disappearing and reappearing on the other side of the field. During replays it's painfully obvious that the netcode isn't quite there yet. What I see on my screen before the goal is scored vs what is replayed are two completely different realities; players in different positions, doing different things, ball nowhere near where it was in real-play, etc. 

100+ ping on local servers is outrageous. Allowing players from the other side of the world to queue into my servers with 500+ ping is outrageous. This game is genuinely unplayable above 50 ping, because what you see and what is happening are two very different things at that point.

Fields of bugs too. Other team scored? Cool, now they start with the ball again and are able to shoot before the keeper can even move. Won the game? Enjoy your desktop background!

This game is nowhere near ready for release. Feels like an Alpha test still. Literally nothing about the actual gameplay has been improved in any way. The only change from the previous test is the addition of a prologue (tutorial) with patchy audio, and paid microtransactions with a FOMO system to boot. Screams "MONEY GRAB" to me.

i9-9900K, GTX 4090, 64gb 6000mz Ram and 1000Mbps down/500Mbps up... The problem ain't on my end.

6/18/25: Today's update makes the game even worse! Now only in 1 of 5 attempts will the game actually launch. And once you're in the game your controller will probably stop working midway through the first game. Have fun!

7/2/25: Servers are even worse now! Every single game is plagued by this awful latency and abysmal netcode. I literally cannot trust my eyes.

[b] 3. [/b]  Кастомизация хорошая, донат не требует, можно спокойно накопить на любую шмотку, при этом выбора достаточно. Плюс есть БП, за вменяемую цену или если вы брали расширенное издание, то вам дадут весь бп бесплатно. Порадовало еще то, что можно менять и само поле, их в достатке.

[b] 4. [/b] Приятная картинка и графика, но тут есть нюанс о котором расскажу в минусах. 

[h1] Минусы [/h1]

[b] 1. [/b] [h2] ВАЖНО! [/h2] По началу я не смог даже поиграть нормально. Постоянно отключало от сервера с ошибкой "Вы были отключены от сетевых сервисов Sloclap". Долго ломал голову, и ДНС сервер менял и шаманил с антивирусом, даже звонил провайдеру, ничего не помогло. Но потом решил попробовать запустить GearUP, и о боже, хотя бы с ним заработало, при этом с пингом все ок (Кстати, для Rematch он бесплатный). Это конечно бред, но видимо таковы реалии, только так и поиграть.

[b] 2. [/b] Нюанс о картинке. Бывает периодически рваные анимации. Вроде ты даешь пасс тиммейту, а мяч улетает за три пи, сами знаете, хотя изначально у тебя на экране мяч летел туда, куда нужно. Это бывает раздражает, надеюсь, что пофиксят. 

[b] 3. [/b] Комьюнити. Не скажу за всех, но большинство людей прям так и хотят забить сами гол, хотя видно, что шансов нет, и если бы они дали пасс, гол был бы супер легким. Пока что мало тимплея, хотя, поиграв в рейтинге, там дела уже обстоят получше.

[b] 4. [/b] Не сказал бы, что это минус, но тут каждый сам решит. Игра прям скилло-зависима. Нужно потренироваться обращаться с мячом, как отбирать его и давать пасс. Потребуется время, чтобы обучиться этому, ибо это очень важно.

This game is an absolute mess.

The server desync is so bad it makes defending feel completely pointless. You’ll make the perfect read, go into the defensive animation, and even see the ball on your screen sitting right at your feet for a split second only for it to instantly warp to the opponent because apparently, they had it the entire time. It’s infuriating. You’re not defending you’re guessing, and then watching the game gaslight you in real time.

To make things worse, when you lose a point, the game is supposed to give you possession. But sometimes it just doesn’t. The other team gets the ball again and you’re back on defense, punished twice in a row, stuck in a loop of broken mechanics and unfair advantages.

And all of this for $50. This game has the polish of an early access demo but the price tag of a finished AAA title. It plays like a tech demo running on netcode powered by a hamster in a microwave.

Save your money and wait for the game to become free to play, or cheaper.

O jogo é maravilhoso, a gameplay/controles do jogo é meio dificil de pegar no inicio e aparenta ser bastante complexa (porque de certo modo, é mesmo), mas quando você pega o jeito, a gameplay fica maravilhosa e muito gostosa de se jogar, posso dizer que to bastante viciado até neste joguinho
Entretanto, o jogo ainda tem vários problemas/conteúdo faltando, por exemplo:

- Bugs

 A maioria dos bugs que eu  tive não atrapalharam nem um pouco a gameplay, e pelo o que eu vejo eles já até estão concertando alguns desses, mas ainda é muito estranho alguns bugs que apareceram na beta do jogo ainda estarem nele (além de novos). Eu até agora tive 2 crash (um foi durante uma partida e outro foi no final dela), esses crashs me deixaram bastante puto.

- Pouca Customização

 Eu achei a customização do jogo com pouca coisa (pelo menos por enquanto), assim tem várias coisas para customizar o personagem, mas a maioria já estava na beta, eu acreditava que quando o jogo lançasse tivesse uma CACETADA de coisas pra customizar o boneco, seja caracteristicas fisicas, roupas, emotes e etc, era umas das coisas que tava mais ansioso pra ver na versão final, uma pena que ainda tenhamos poucos itens de customização.

- Servidores Instaveis

 Os servidores do jogo ainda estão bastantes instaveis, tem vezes que a partida inteira fica normal e lisinha, e outras que o servidor começa a "engasgar", dificultando em certos momentos da jogatina.

No geral, o jogo ainda tem bastante problemas, acredito e ESPERO que todos estes problemas sejam resolvidos com o tempo, entretanto o jogo é extremamente divertido, frenético e show de bola, perfeito pra jogar sozinho ou com amigos, recomendo demais esse jogo, mas caso ainda esteja apreensivo sobre ele, é melhor esperar até que vários destes problemas sejam resolvidos antes de comprar o jogo.

Un excellent jeu de foot avec beaucoup de potentiel !

Rematch est un jeu vraiment fun et addictif, avec une bonne dose d’originalité. On sent qu’il a été pensé avec passion. Le gameplay est très plaisant, et chaque match donne envie d’enchaîner. C’est clairement un jeu que je recommande à tous les fans de foot arcade !

Cela dit, il reste encore pas mal de bugs et d’ajustements à faire. Certaines mécaniques pourraient être mieux équilibrées, et quelques soucis techniques viennent parfois casser un peu l’expérience.

Mais je reste confiant : les développeurs semblent impliqués, et je pense qu’avec un peu de temps, ils corrigeront tout ça. Le jeu a un énorme potentiel, et je suis impatient de voir comment il va évoluer !

**Still the same laggy mess since beta.**

Nothing has changed.
Servers are terrible, hit detection is off, and the overall gameplay feels sloppy.

**Design decisions make zero sense.**
Normal mode lets you pick 3v3, 4v4, or 5v5 
but *ranked* is **only 5v5** with **no restriction** on premades?

Why is there no solo-only ranked mode?
Solo queue players get destroyed by full squads constantly.
Ranked should have two queues:

* **Solo 5v5 (no premades)**
* **Premade 5v5 (separate rank)**

**Beta played smoother than this.**
You could dribble, outplay, and actually control the ball.
Now it’s just who lags better in 1v1 fights.
Ball physics and possession feel like random server flips.

Every game still shows “first win of the day XP” 
a known visual bug that somehow still exists.

**Fun idea, terrible execution.**
There’s *no solo-only mode* in the entire game.
Playing without a team is frustrating and unbalanced.

Feels like the devs only test on LAN 
because no way they think the current online experience is fine.

I know it’s only Season 0 
but some of these issues are so basic, it’s wild they aren’t fixed.

In the current state - AVOID!

Right now the game is very unstable, crashes and lags are constant.

On top of that there's A LOT of game breaking bugs:
Sometimes you pass the ball at the start of a round to your teammate, and it teleports across the field to opposite goalkeeper.
Sometimes the goalkeeper catches the ball already inside the net and it counts as a goal, and sometimes it doesn't count.
The games sometimes start without the full teams on both sides.
Controllers sometimes doen't work after the game begins. 
The match starts even when matchmaking was cancelled. 
The keyboard steering and aiming doesn't fully work.

We paid the developer to be able to play it early, and in return we got a half assed product.

This game would be great without the terrible desync. This is coming from someone who put up 40hrs on the playtests too so not a hater.

edit: After a long time not playing i come back to see this game is struggling to keep a player count. The main reason i believe is the lack of new content outside cosmetics, as well as the bugs and network errors. If they want to revive this game: buckle down and lock in on fixing the bugs; Go back into full dev mode instead of leaving the game to rot, Add new content such as new mechanics and quickly cycling limited time modes to keep people engaged long term.

 and finally make the game free to play. The devs have made the most money they ever will from having the game be paid, now its time to reward the paid players for paying and make the game free for all, obviously keeping the cosmetics shop so they can make a good amount of money especially with more players. We saw the player count spike when the game went free for a weekend, keep it that way and even if the player count stays at half that amount it would be far from dead.

After SIFU I thought the devs are fairly competent. Oh boy, how wrong I was.

Let's start from the fact that the game is absolutely unfriendly to play as a solo player. Even if you play well yourself it does not matter cause it's rely so much on teamwork that solo you have close to no impact of the final result of the game. Teammates don't pass? Well, you got a problem. If you do not have friends to play with - do not even consider buying this game.

You have friends to play with? Read further. The servers and netcode are basically the worst thing you can imagine for an online game. Literally the worst. The cheapest server provider in the world was chosen 100%. Constant desync. And I mean CONSTANT. Game crashing, kicking you out of servers during the match back to the lobby and such are a norm.

And the best part is that even after 10 years after Rocket League came up and shown how client-server should be handled this game sends more info than just inputs from the client to the server so cheaters are rampant.

ソロでマスターまでは頑張ったがアンインストールした。
結論から言うと５人の友達を用意できないなら買わない方が良い。

ゲーム自体は奥深く面白いものだが、それを支える土台と環境がまだ整ってない。

一番クソなのはゲーム体験を不快にさせる味方内での煽りや利敵行為や萎え落ちなどの有害行為があり、これを咎めたり排除したり補填するシステムが一切ないこと。

通報はDISCORDまで飛ぶ必要あり、そこから先もゲームの有害行為を通報するだけなのにかなり手間な手順が待っている(ユーザー名でなくID、スクショなどの証拠、詳細説明、自分のDISCORDのアカウントは電話番号と紐付け必要)。こんな感じでただ不快なプレイヤーの対処をしたいだけなのにハードルが高いものとなっている。要するに、現在は無法地帯ってこと。

開発人数が少ないからこういう手段を取ってるのかもしれないが、それで許されることでもない。

また、他のレビューにもある通り、フルパが強いためランクが上がれば上がるほどパーティに遭遇しやすい。マスターまで来ると基本パーティだらけ。そうなるとそのパーティが味方の場合は疎外感あるし(パスくれないとか)、敵であれば連携取りまくってるので強い。自然とソロの限界を感じ始める。

ソロはソロだけのマッチングなどにしないとソロ勢はどんどんやめていくと思う。実際、同時接続数を見ると右肩下がりで過疎化がガンガン進んでいる。

ゲーム体験としては瞬間的には面白いものを提供してもらったが、残念ながらこの環境では他人にオススメはできないという判断になる。ゲーム自体は面白いのに、環境整備の甘さでゲームの寿命を縮めている。

一个非常影响平衡的bug，而且出现率很高：有时候进球之后不在中圈开球，却在对方球员的脚底下开球，中圈开球的那个踢空气。。。希望官方能修一下。

还有几个建议：1、最大的问题是传球操作，用键鼠的话，这个传球系统就太难瞄准了，基本拿到球都不爱传球，而且传球力度条不 
                              够明显，长传不好把握力度，并且蓄力的时候才看得到传球方向
                        2、按下射门键之后，蓄力的时候就不能改射门方向了，希望按下射门键后能继续瞄准，不过这条也能接受
                        3、游戏推荐用手柄玩，但手柄不方便射门和移动视角，用键鼠玩又不方便传球，希望操作能改改，不然都玩得难受
                        4、有人退了之后只能少打多，根本打不过，希望以后队友退了之后能加入人机队友，最好是能出个人机模式，或者 
                             单人战役模式
                        5、中国区的网络连接质量太差了，不开加速器玩不了

Positivo:

Começando o game é difícil, por que a questão de controlar a câmera, e mirar pro gol é difícil no controle, não joguei no mouse e teclado, mas depois de um tempo fica de boa.

A mecânica de drible é fácil, não tem um combo pra cada drible igual no FIFA, e são poucos comando pra aprender . Partidas não são rápidas e dinâmicas, e se você joga com ozamigo vai gerar muita resenha.

Negativos:

Algumas partidas mesmo no serv SA, lagavam, ou um dos membros do time não entrava e era substituído por alguém aleatório ou bots, no caso joguei com 3 amigos e as vezes um dos mesmo não entrava na partida.

O goleiro defendia algumas bolas já dentro do gol ( fui favorecido algumas vezes não posso reclamar kkkkkk )
Devido ao lag, tinha jogadas que dava Teleport.

E já tinha jogada bugada, onde o goleiro tinha que ser lenda pra pegar, porque era indefensável.

No beta, não dava pra mudar os controles, entao isso limitava a gameplay, e jogadores de outros jogos de futebas podem se confundir com botões.

[h1]Divertido, ótima jogabilidade, um pouco repetitivo e com players fominha, não tem falta(futebol de cadeia!).[/h1]
[list]
[*] Enfrentando crashes as vezes, mesmo com um PC bem Highend. (não acontecia na beta)
[*] O netcode não é dos melhores, e jogadores usam disso para meio que bugar o jogo. Tem melhorado, mais ainda precisa de ajustes. E raramente, o seu personagem pode perder a colisão com a bola, só resolve reiniciando o jogo.
[*] Poucos dribles.
[*] Players fominhas ou banheira estragando sua partida. (MUITO COMUM)
[*] Jogabilidade interessante, mas presa a um "envelope".
[*] Te deixa livre para criar jogadas e ter um dinamismo maior em relação aos jogos normais de futebol, se a equipe jogar bem.
[*] A bola não sai, então o pessoal fica fazendo tabela com a parede igual rocket league. =/
[*] Não tem falta e o carrinho ta meio bugado.
[/list]

Mesmo que eu afirme que o jogo é repetitivo, a certo ponto ele ainda é divertido e eu recomendo por isso. Eu não sou fan de jogos de futebol como FIFA e PES, mas esse jogo é bem divertido pelo fato que só tem players e não aqueles bots zoados, ou uma paywall enorme comprando jogadores. Mas ao mesmo tempo isso é estragado porque você depende de outros jogadores. Dessa maneira o jogo é bem legal quando você cai em um time que joga bem. Os dribles e a mecânica te deixam um pouco preso a uma mecânica X, talvez no futuro entre outras mecânicas. Até o momento tudo é balanceado e bom. Só é um pouco chato a tabela na linha de fundo. Infelizmente os devs fizeram algo de errado antes do lançamento, o jogo está crashando com uma frequencia bem alta até. Meu PC é bom(vc encontra a config no meu perfil), então isso não deveria acontecer. Quando não no meio da partida vc cai e volta para o menu. Parece que a empresa dona do jogo está com um backend bem ruim.

Em suma recomendo a todos, o jogo é divertido e desafiador ao mesmo tempo. O unico problema é o netcode e os jogadores. Se você tiver amigo para fechar um time, vai amar esse jogo.

I have 50+ hours in the playtest, I will 100% recommend this game. However I would like to state that the servers are in a horrid state. At first I thought it was just my crap internet but everyone is experiencing some form of latency issue. I will list some of the current pros and cons

Pros 

- Highly competitive with an extreme emphasis on teamplay
- Fun and rewarding mechanics that make you feel pure bliss when executed correctly
- Built in training system and quick access to freeplay with the press of a button, offering tons of ways to build different skills
- Customizable characters. There aren't a ton of options yet, but I love being able to make my dude with enough freedom to make him unique
- Toggleable voice chat. I just like being able to quickly press LT to either call out hot mic'ing toxic players or to praise my teammates

Cons 

- Servers are dog water currently
- Not a whole lot of variety in game modes, making me concerned for replayability
- Sometimes the controls feel unresponsive but that could just be the servers
- Only a few hours in and four crashes
- There is a bug where the scoring team will have possession of the ball at the start of the next kickoff leading to very often unfair games
- Interceptions are inconsistent, especially if two opposing players try to intercept at the same time. This can often lead to the ball bugging out and remaining static

Overall Rematch is very solid and I think it's going to be my main competitive game for a long time to come. I expect frequent updates as Sloclap seems very responsive to their community
